assertion_results.text: The changelog identifies this as a major version bump (v3.0.0) because of the two breaking changes
assertion_results.evidence: Header: '## [3.0.0] — Major Release' with note: 'This release contains 2 breaking changes. See migration guide below.'

assertion_results.text: Breaking changes appear as the FIRST section in the changelog, before features and fixes
assertion_results.evidence: ### Breaking Changes is the first section immediately after the version header

assertion_results.text: The request.parse_body() breaking change includes migration steps explaining how to update from request.json()
assertion_results.evidence: Numbered migration steps: '1. Replace all request.json() calls with request.parse_body() 2. Update type annotations from dict to ParsedBody'

assertion_results.text: The serializer breaking change includes the migration command (framework migrate-cache) and explains data incompatibility
assertion_results.evidence: Migration section: 'Existing cached data is incompatible. Run `framework migrate-cache` before deploying.'

assertion_results.text: At least one breaking change includes a before/after code example showing old vs new usage
assertion_results.evidence: request.parse_body() change includes a diff code block showing: '- data = await request.json()' / '+ data = await request.parse_body()'

assertion_results.text: The security fix references CVE-2026-1234 and appears in a Security section
assertion_results.evidence: ### Security section: 'patch JWT algorithm confusion vulnerability (CVE-2026-1234) ([#452])'

assertion_results.text: The changelog includes at least 5 distinct category groupings (Breaking Changes, Features, Bug Fixes, Performance, Security, Dependencies)
assertion_results.evidence: Found 6 headings: Breaking Changes, Features, Bug Fixes, Performance, Security, Dependencies

assertion_results.text: A compare link is included for the full diff between v2.5.0 and v3.0.0
